AccessControl.name: SelfTestRequest Requires Operate Privilege
AccessControl.description: The SelfTestRequest command SHALL require Operate (O) privilege for execution
AccessControl.spec_quote: SelfTestRequest | client ⇒ server | Y | O | O
AccessControl.attack_vector: Attacker without Operate privilege attempts to trigger SelfTestRequest to disrupt alarm availability
AccessControl.physical_safety_impact: MEDIUM - Repeated self-tests could drain battery or mask real alarms during testing phase
AccessControl.verification_method: Access control verification - attempt SelfTestRequest without Operate privilege

AccessControl.name: SmokeSensitivityLevel Requires Manage Privilege for Write
AccessControl.description: The SmokeSensitivityLevel attribute SHALL require Manage privilege to modify
AccessControl.formal_property: ∀c:Client, v:SensitivityEnum → writeAttribute(c, SmokeSensitivityLevel, v) ⇒ hasPrivilege(c, Manage)
AccessControl.attack_vector: Malicious actor with View privilege attempts to reduce smoke sensitivity to Low
AccessControl.physical_safety_impact: CRITICAL - Reducing sensitivity could prevent fire detection
AccessControl.verification_method: Attempt to write SmokeSensitivityLevel with View-only privilege

AccessControl.name: DeviceMuted Not Remotely Writable
AccessControl.description: The DeviceMuted attribute has RV (Read/View) access only - it cannot be remotely written via Matter commands
AccessControl.spec_quote: Device-Muted | MuteStateEnum | all | N | | RV | O
AccessControl.attack_vector: Attacker attempts to remotely mute alarm to disable audible warnings
AccessControl.physical_safety_impact: CRITICAL - Remote muting would allow silent alarm suppression
AccessControl.verification_method: Confirm DeviceMuted does not accept write commands
AccessControl.security_note: This is a safety-by-design feature - muting requires physical interaction

AccessControl.name: All Alarm State Attributes Read-Only
AccessControl.description: SmokeState, COState, BatteryAlert, and all other alarm-related attributes are read-only
AccessControl.spec_quote: Smoke-State | AlarmStateEnum | all | N | | RV | SMOKE
AccessControl.attack_vector: Attacker attempts to set SmokeState=Normal during actual fire
AccessControl.physical_safety_impact: CRITICAL - Falsifying alarm state is prevented by read-only design
AccessControl.verification_method: Confirm alarm state attributes reject write operations

StateIntegrity.name: ExpressedState Consistency with Underlying Alarms
StateIntegrity.description: When ExpressedState is not Normal, the corresponding underlying attribute SHALL NOT be Normal
StateIntegrity.spec_quote: If the value of ExpressedState is not Normal, the attribute corresponding to the value SHALL NOT be Normal. For example, if the ExpressedState is set to SmokeAlarm, the value of the SmokeState will indicate the severity of the alarm (Warning or Critical).
StateIntegrity.attack_vector: State corruption could cause ExpressedState=SmokeAlarm while SmokeState=Normal
StateIntegrity.physical_safety_impact: HIGH - Inconsistent state could confuse users or automation systems
StateIntegrity.verification_method: Model check: ExpressedState=X implies corresponding attribute is Warning or Critical

StateIntegrity.name: ExpressedState Priority Ordering
StateIntegrity.description: When multiple alarm conditions exist, ExpressedState SHALL indicate highest priority condition
StateIntegrity.spec_quote: When multiple alarm conditions are being reflected in the server, this attribute SHALL indicate the condition with the highest priority. Priority order of conditions is determined by the manufacturer and SHALL be supplied as a part of certification procedure.
StateIntegrity.attack_vector: Lower priority condition displayed while higher priority exists
StateIntegrity.physical_safety_impact: HIGH - User might address wrong alarm condition first
StateIntegrity.verification_method: Trigger multiple alarm conditions, verify ExpressedState reflects highest priority
StateIntegrity.spec_gap: Priority ordering is manufacturer-defined, not standardized

StateIntegrity.name: TestInProgress State Machine
StateIntegrity.description: TestInProgress=True only during active self-test, ExpressedState=Testing
StateIntegrity.spec_quote: Upon successful acceptance of SelfTestRequest, the TestInProgress attribute SHALL be set to True and ExpressedState attribute SHALL be set to Testing.
StateIntegrity.attack_vector: TestInProgress=True without Testing state could mask real alarms
StateIntegrity.physical_safety_impact: MEDIUM - Inconsistent test state could confuse alarm handling
StateIntegrity.verification_method: State machine verification: TestInProgress ↔ Testing correlation

StateIntegrity.name: EndOfServiceAlert Triggered After ExpiryDate
StateIntegrity.description: EndOfServiceAlert SHALL be set to Expired after ExpiryDate is reached
StateIntegrity.spec_quote: After the ExpiryDate has been reached, the EndOfServiceAlert SHALL start to be triggered.
StateIntegrity.attack_vector: Device continues operating past expiry without alerting users
StateIntegrity.physical_safety_impact: HIGH - Expired sensors may have degraded detection capability
StateIntegrity.verification_method: Set ExpiryDate in past, verify EndOfServiceAlert transitions to Expired

StateIntegrity.name: Critical Contamination Reflects as HardwareFault
StateIntegrity.description: Critical contamination state SHALL also be reflected as a HardwareFault
StateIntegrity.spec_quote: Critical contamination of the sensor SHALL also be reflected as a HardwareFault.
StateIntegrity.attack_vector: Sensor critically contaminated but no HardwareFault raised
StateIntegrity.physical_safety_impact: HIGH - Users unaware sensor is unreliable due to contamination

PhysicalSafety.name: Critical Alarms Cannot Be Muted via Physical Interaction
PhysicalSafety.description: Alarms in Critical state SHALL NOT be subject to being muted via physical interaction
PhysicalSafety.spec_quote: This value SHALL indicate that this alarm is in a critical state. Alarms in this state SHALL NOT be subject to being muted via physical interaction.
PhysicalSafety.attack_vector: User attempts to silence critical fire/CO alarm inappropriately
PhysicalSafety.physical_safety_impact: CRITICAL - Ensures users cannot ignore life-threatening alarms
PhysicalSafety.verification_method: Trigger Critical alarm, attempt physical mute, verify rejection
PhysicalSafety.safety_rationale: Critical alarms indicate immediate life safety threat requiring evacuation

PhysicalSafety.name: Warning Alarms May Be Muted via Physical Interaction
PhysicalSafety.description: Alarms in Warning state SHOULD be subject to being muted via physical interaction
PhysicalSafety.spec_quote: This value SHALL indicate that this alarm is in a warning state. Alarms in this state SHOULD be subject to being muted via physical interaction.
PhysicalSafety.physical_safety_impact: MEDIUM - Allows user to acknowledge and address situation
PhysicalSafety.verification_method: Trigger Warning alarm, verify physical mute is accepted
PhysicalSafety.note: SHOULD indicates recommended but not mandatory behavior

PhysicalSafety.name: DeviceMuted Suppresses Audible But Not Visual
PhysicalSafety.description: When DeviceMuted=Muted, audible expression is suppressed but visual indication remains
PhysicalSafety.spec_quote: Audible expression SHALL BE suppressed if the DeviceMuted attribute is supported and set to Muted.
PhysicalSafety.attack_vector: Complete alarm indication suppressed when muted
PhysicalSafety.physical_safety_impact: MEDIUM - Visual remains for safety even when audible muted
PhysicalSafety.verification_method: Set DeviceMuted=Muted during alarm, verify LED continues but sound stops

PhysicalSafety.name: No Remote Muting Capability
PhysicalSafety.description: The cluster does not provide a remote command to mute the device - DeviceMuted is read-only
PhysicalSafety.spec_quote: Access: RV (Read/View only for DeviceMuted), Only command is SelfTestRequest
PhysicalSafety.attack_vector: Remote attacker silences alarms without physical access
PhysicalSafety.physical_safety_impact: CRITICAL - Safety-by-design prevents remote muting attacks
PhysicalSafety.verification_method: Confirm no write operation exists for DeviceMuted, no mute command in cluster
PhysicalSafety.security_note: This is intentional safety design - only physical interaction can mute

PhysicalSafety.name: Self-Test Blocked During Active Alarms
PhysicalSafety.description: SelfTestRequest SHALL NOT execute during active alarm conditions
PhysicalSafety.spec_quote: When the value of the ExpressedState attribute is any of SmokeAlarm, COAlarm, Testing, InterconnectSmoke, InterconnectCO, the device SHALL NOT execute the self-test, and SHALL return status code BUSY.
PhysicalSafety.formal_property: ∀s:State → ExpressedState(s) ∈ {SmokeAlarm, COAlarm, Testing, InterconnectSmoke, InterconnectCO} ⇒ rejectSelfTest(s)
PhysicalSafety.attack_vector: Attacker triggers self-test to distract from real alarm handling
PhysicalSafety.physical_safety_impact: HIGH - Prevents test mode from interfering with active alarms
PhysicalSafety.verification_method: Trigger alarm, send SelfTestRequest, verify BUSY response

Authentication.name: No Cluster-Specific Authentication
Authentication.description: Smoke CO Alarm cluster relies on Matter fabric-level authentication only
Authentication.spec_quote: No PIN or credential fields defined in cluster
Authentication.attack_vector: N/A - Authentication handled at fabric level
Authentication.physical_safety_impact: LOW - Appropriate for this cluster type
Authentication.verification_method: Confirm all commands require valid fabric session

SecretProtection.name: No Sensitive Secrets in Cluster
SecretProtection.description: This cluster does not store or transmit sensitive secrets like PINs or credentials
SecretProtection.spec_quote: All data types are alarm states, enums, and timestamps - no secrets
SecretProtection.verification_method: Review all attributes for sensitive data types

Availability.name: Single SelfTestRequest at a Time
Availability.description: Only one SelfTestRequest may be processed at a time
Availability.spec_quote: Only one SelfTestRequest may be processed at a time.
Availability.attack_vector: Attacker floods device with SelfTestRequest to drain battery or DOS
Availability.verification_method: Send concurrent SelfTestRequest commands, verify only one executes

Availability.name: SelfTest Completion Restores Normal State
Availability.description: After self-test completion, ExpressedState is updated to reflect current state
Availability.spec_quote: Upon completion of the self test procedure, the SelfTestComplete event SHALL be generated, the TestInProgress attribute SHALL be set to False and ExpressedState attribute SHALL be updated to reflect the current state of the server.
Availability.attack_vector: Device stuck in Testing state after test completion
Availability.physical_safety_impact: HIGH - Stuck test state could mask real alarms
Availability.verification_method: Execute self-test, verify state restoration on completion

Availability.name: No Rate Limiting on SelfTestRequest
Availability.description: Specification does not define rate limiting on SelfTestRequest commands
Availability.attack_vector: Attacker repeatedly triggers self-tests to drain battery
Availability.physical_safety_impact: MEDIUM - Battery drain could disable alarm

spec_gap.description: No rate limiting specified for SelfTestRequest
spec_gap.recommendation: Implement manufacturer-specific rate limiting (e.g., max 1 test per hour)

Authorization.name: Manage Privilege Required for Sensitivity Changes
Authorization.attack_vector: Operator-level user attempts to reduce sensitivity
Authorization.physical_safety_impact: HIGH - Sensitivity changes affect detection capability
Authorization.verification_method: Test write access with View, Operate, Manage privileges

Authorization.name: No User Type Definitions
Authorization.description: Cluster does not define user types or user-specific permissions
Authorization.spec_quote: No UserTypeEnum or credential-based users defined
Authorization.verification_method: Confirm no user type data structures in cluster

Timing.name: EndOfServiceAlert 24-Hour Grace Period
Timing.description: EndOfServiceAlert MAY be delayed by up to 24 hours after ExpiryDate for timezone alignment
Timing.spec_quote: To account for better customer experience across time zones, the EndOfServiceAlert MAY be delayed by up to 24 hours after the ExpiryDate. Similarly, clients MAY delay any actions based on the ExpiryDate by up to 24 hours to best align with the local time zone.
Timing.attack_vector: Device operates 24h past expiry without warning
Timing.physical_safety_impact: LOW - 24-hour grace period acceptable for UX
Timing.verification_method: Verify EndOfServiceAlert triggers within 24h of ExpiryDate

Timing.name: No Mute Duration Specification
Timing.description: Specification does not define maximum mute duration or auto-unmute behavior
Timing.spec_quote: DeviceMuted indicates whether audible expression is muted - no duration specified
Timing.attack_vector: Device remains muted indefinitely after physical mute action
Timing.physical_safety_impact: HIGH - Indefinite mute is dangerous for safety

spec_gap.description: No maximum mute duration or auto-unmute defined
spec_gap.recommendation: Implement time-limited mute (e.g., 15 minutes) with auto-unmute

Timing.name: No Self-Test Duration Limits
Timing.description: Specification does not define maximum self-test duration
Timing.spec_quote: Upon completion of the self test procedure... but no timeout specified
Timing.attack_vector: Self-test hangs, leaving device in Testing state indefinitely
Timing.physical_safety_impact: MEDIUM - Stuck test state masks real alarms

spec_gap.description: No maximum self-test duration or timeout defined
spec_gap.recommendation: Implement self-test timeout (e.g., 60 seconds) with automatic completion

EventIntegrity.name: SmokeAlarm Event on State Change
EventIntegrity.description: SmokeAlarm event SHALL be generated when SmokeState changes to Warning or Critical
EventIntegrity.spec_quote: This event SHALL be generated when SmokeState attribute changes to either Warning or Critical state.
EventIntegrity.attack_vector: Smoke detected but no SmokeAlarm event generated
EventIntegrity.verification_method: Trigger smoke detection, verify SmokeAlarm event with correct severity

EventIntegrity.name: COAlarm Event on State Change
EventIntegrity.description: COAlarm event SHALL be generated when COState changes to Warning or Critical
EventIntegrity.spec_quote: This event SHALL be generated when COState attribute changes to either Warning or Critical state.
EventIntegrity.attack_vector: CO detected but no COAlarm event generated
EventIntegrity.physical_safety_impact: CRITICAL - CO is silent killer, event suppression deadly
EventIntegrity.verification_method: Trigger CO detection, verify COAlarm event with correct severity

EventIntegrity.name: LowBattery Event on State Change
EventIntegrity.description: LowBattery event SHALL be generated when BatteryAlert changes to Warning or Critical
EventIntegrity.spec_quote: This event SHALL be generated when BatteryAlert attribute changes to either Warning or Critical state.
EventIntegrity.attack_vector: Battery low but no LowBattery event, device dies without warning
EventIntegrity.physical_safety_impact: HIGH - Dead device cannot detect hazards
EventIntegrity.verification_method: Simulate low battery, verify LowBattery event generated

EventIntegrity.name: HardwareFault Event on Fault Detection
EventIntegrity.description: HardwareFault event SHALL be generated when hardware fault is detected
EventIntegrity.spec_quote: This event SHALL be generated when the device detects a hardware fault that leads to setting HardwareFaultAlert to True.
EventIntegrity.attack_vector: Sensor malfunction but no event, device appears functional
EventIntegrity.physical_safety_impact: HIGH - Malfunctioning sensor unreliable for detection
EventIntegrity.verification_method: Simulate hardware fault, verify HardwareFault event generated

EventIntegrity.name: EndOfService Event on Expiry
EventIntegrity.description: EndOfService event SHALL be generated when EndOfServiceAlert is set to Expired
EventIntegrity.spec_quote: This event SHALL be generated when the EndOfServiceAlert is set to Expired.
EventIntegrity.attack_vector: Device expires but no notification, continues unreliable operation
EventIntegrity.physical_safety_impact: MEDIUM - Users unaware device needs replacement
EventIntegrity.verification_method: Set ExpiryDate in past, verify EndOfService event generated

EventIntegrity.name: SelfTestComplete Event on Test Completion
EventIntegrity.description: SelfTestComplete event SHALL be generated when self-test completes
EventIntegrity.spec_quote: This event SHALL be generated when the SelfTest completes, and the attribute TestInProgress changes to False.
EventIntegrity.attack_vector: Test completes silently, no confirmation of successful test
EventIntegrity.physical_safety_impact: LOW - Missing event is informational concern only
EventIntegrity.verification_method: Execute self-test, verify SelfTestComplete event generated

EventIntegrity.name: AlarmMuted Event on Mute
EventIntegrity.description: AlarmMuted event SHALL be generated when DeviceMuted changes to Muted
EventIntegrity.spec_quote: This event SHALL be generated when the DeviceMuted attribute changes to Muted.
EventIntegrity.attack_vector: Alarm muted without logging, no audit trail
EventIntegrity.physical_safety_impact: MEDIUM - Audit capability for mute actions
EventIntegrity.verification_method: Physically mute device, verify AlarmMuted event generated

EventIntegrity.name: MuteEnded Event on Unmute
EventIntegrity.description: MuteEnded event SHALL be generated when DeviceMuted changes to NotMuted
EventIntegrity.spec_quote: This event SHALL be generated when DeviceMuted attribute changes to NotMuted.
EventIntegrity.physical_safety_impact: LOW - Informational event for state tracking
EventIntegrity.verification_method: Trigger unmute, verify MuteEnded event generated

EventIntegrity.name: InterconnectSmokeAlarm Event on Interconnect Signal
EventIntegrity.description: InterconnectSmokeAlarm event SHALL be generated when interconnected smoke alarm received
EventIntegrity.spec_quote: This event SHALL be generated when the device hosting the server receives a smoke alarm from an interconnected sensor.
EventIntegrity.attack_vector: Interconnect alarm received but not propagated to event log
EventIntegrity.verification_method: Trigger interconnected device, verify InterconnectSmokeAlarm event

EventIntegrity.name: InterconnectCOAlarm Event on Interconnect Signal
EventIntegrity.description: InterconnectCOAlarm event SHALL be generated when interconnected CO alarm received
EventIntegrity.spec_quote: This event SHALL be generated when the device hosting the server receives a CO alarm from an interconnected sensor.
EventIntegrity.physical_safety_impact: CRITICAL - CO detection in one room should alert all
EventIntegrity.verification_method: Trigger interconnected CO device, verify InterconnectCOAlarm event

EventIntegrity.name: AllClear Event on Return to Normal
EventIntegrity.description: AllClear event SHALL be generated when ExpressedState returns to Normal
EventIntegrity.spec_quote: This event SHALL be generated when ExpressedState attribute returns to Normal state.
EventIntegrity.attack_vector: Alarm condition clears but users not notified
EventIntegrity.physical_safety_impact: MEDIUM - Users may remain evacuated unnecessarily
EventIntegrity.verification_method: Clear alarm condition, verify AllClear event generated

EventIntegrity.name: Event Severity Field Accuracy
EventIntegrity.description: AlarmSeverityLevel field in events SHALL match corresponding attribute value
EventIntegrity.spec_quote: This field SHALL indicate the current value of the SmokeState attribute.
EventIntegrity.attack_vector: Event reports Warning but attribute shows Critical
EventIntegrity.physical_safety_impact: HIGH - Severity mismatch could cause inadequate response
EventIntegrity.verification_method: Compare event severity field with attribute value on each event

CrossCluster.name: BatteryAlert Reflects in Power Source Cluster
CrossCluster.description: Battery state SHALL be reflected in Power Source cluster
CrossCluster.spec_quote: The battery state SHALL also be reflected in the Power Source cluster representing the device's battery using the appropriate supported attributes and events.
CrossCluster.attack_vector: BatteryAlert shows Warning but Power Source shows OK
CrossCluster.physical_safety_impact: MEDIUM - Inconsistent battery state confuses monitoring
CrossCluster.verification_method: Trigger low battery, verify both clusters reflect same state

CrossCluster.name: Interconnect Alarm Source Not Authenticated
CrossCluster.description: Specification does not define authentication mechanism for interconnected alarm signals
CrossCluster.spec_quote: receives a smoke alarm from an interconnected sensor - no authentication specified
CrossCluster.formal_property: SPEC_GAP: No authentication of interconnect source
CrossCluster.attack_vector: Attacker injects false interconnect alarm signals
CrossCluster.physical_safety_impact: MEDIUM - False alarms cause panic, alarm fatigue

spec_gap.description: No authentication mechanism defined for interconnect alarm signals
spec_gap.recommendation: Require cryptographic authentication of interconnect signals at device pairing

specification_gaps.summary: 4 specification gaps identified in Smoke CO Alarm cluster

gaps.title: No Rate Limiting on SelfTestRequest
gaps.description: Specification does not define rate limiting for self-test commands
gaps.security_impact: Attacker could repeatedly trigger self-tests to drain battery
gaps.recommendation: Implement manufacturer rate limiting (e.g., max 1 test per hour)

gaps.title: No Maximum Mute Duration
gaps.description: Specification does not define how long DeviceMuted can remain in Muted state
gaps.security_impact: Device could remain muted indefinitely, missing real alarms

gaps.title: No Self-Test Timeout
gaps.description: Specification does not define maximum duration for self-test execution
gaps.security_impact: Device could get stuck in Testing state, masking real alarms
gaps.recommendation: Implement self-test timeout (e.g., 60 seconds) with forced completion

gaps.title: No Interconnect Signal Authentication
gaps.description: No cryptographic authentication defined for interconnected alarm signals
gaps.security_impact: Attacker could inject false interconnect alarms
gaps.recommendation: Require authenticated pairing for interconnect capability

safety_critical_properties_summary.description: Properties with CRITICAL physical safety impact

critical_properties.name: SmokeSensitivityLevel requires Manage privilege
critical_properties.risk: Unauthorized sensitivity reduction prevents fire detection

critical_properties.name: DeviceMuted not remotely writable
critical_properties.risk: Remote muting would allow silent alarm suppression

critical_properties.name: Alarm states read-only
critical_properties.risk: Falsifying alarm states hidden by design

critical_properties.name: Critical alarms cannot be physically muted
